evals.prompt: I have a CHIRPS precipitation xarray Dataset loaded as `ds_chirps` (dims: lat, lon, time) and a SoilGrids soil organic carbon Dataset loaded as `ds_soil` (dims: lat, lon, no time). I want to clip both to Ethiopia (ISO3: ETH) at admin level 1 (regions), then stack them into a single cube at the coarsest resolution. Write the masking and stacking functions inline into my script.
evals.expected_output: Two functions written inline: mask_to_admin() clipping each dataset to ETH admin level 1 via GADM, and stack_datasets() merging them at coarsest resolution. Both functions include STAC-compliant attributes. Static soil layer handled gracefully alongside time-varying CHIRPS.
assertions: mask_to_admin function is present with country_iso3, admin_level, and shapefile_path parameters
GADM URL uses ETH and admin level 1 in the example call or comment
stack_datasets function is present with target_resolution='coarsest' as default or argument
ds.rio.reproject_match is used for spatial alignment
xr.merge is used to combine datasets
STAC attributes (bbox, proj:epsg, instruments, platform) are set on output datasets
Static layer (soil, no time dim) is mentioned or handled gracefully

evals.prompt: I've got a masked xarray Dataset `ds_masked` covering Colombia (COL) with variables 'precipitation' and 'temperature' and a time dimension (daily, 2020–2022). I have a GeoDataFrame `gdf_departments` with Colombia's departments loaded from a local shapefile. Write me a summarize_by_admin function that computes monthly mean precipitation and temperature per department and saves results to 'colombia_monthly_stats.csv'.
evals.expected_output: summarize_by_admin function written inline that clips to each department, resamples to monthly frequency using xr.resample(time='ME'), computes mean, and outputs a tidy DataFrame saved to the specified CSV path. Columns: admin_unit, variable, time, value.
assertions: summarize_by_admin function is present with boundaries, agg_method, temporal_freq, and output_csv parameters
xr.resample with time='ME' or temporal_freq='ME' is used for monthly aggregation
rio.clip is used to iterate over admin units
output DataFrame has admin_unit, variable, time, value columns (mentioned or demonstrated)
output_csv='colombia_monthly_stats.csv' is passed in the example call
has_time check is present to handle static vs time-varying layers

evals.prompt: I'm working with three raster datasets in Python: a DEM (elevation, static, 90m resolution), an AgERA5 solar radiation dataset (daily, 0.1 degree resolution), and a CHIRPS rainfall dataset (daily, 0.05 degree resolution). All are loaded as xarray Datasets with lat/lon dims. I want to stack them into one cube at the finest resolution, then export each variable as a separate Cloud Optimized GeoTIFF. Write the stacking function and the COG export code.
evals.expected_output: stack_datasets function written with target_resolution='finest', plus a loop or per-variable code block calling .rio.to_raster(driver='COG') for each variable in the merged dataset. STAC attributes included on the stacked output.
assertions: stack_datasets function is present
target_resolution='finest' is used in the example call
rio.reproject_match is used for alignment
rio.to_raster with driver='COG' is present for export
COG export iterates over data variables or shows per-variable export
STAC attributes (bbox, proj:epsg, instruments) are set on the stacked dataset
Static DEM layer (no time dim) is mentioned as handled or included in merge
